title: Xtra Roasted Portuguese Kongnamul Roast
description: A pescatarian friendly Portuguese dish featuring roasted soybean sprouts and tempered chocolate, served on a slate tile.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toss the soybean sprouts with olive oil, garlic, portuguese sea salt, and black pepper in a bowl until they are evenly coated.
Spread the soybean sprouts on a slate tile in a single layer.
Roast the soybean sprouts in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
Melt the tempered chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30 second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Stir the lemon juice into the melted chocolate until well combined.
Brush the chocolate mixture over the roasted soybean sprouts.
Top the soybean sprouts with grilled shrimp.
Return the slate tile to the oven and roast for an additional 5 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Remove the slate tile from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
